tasks.name: N3: error gen and checker testing in the top level
tasks.description:
**TestPlan:**
1. Testcase where errors in only 1 field is enabled (5 testcases, one for each field, excluding start\_sequence errors).
2. Testcase with error in:
    1. PAYLOAD and SIZE.
    2. PAYLOAD, SIZE and ID.
    3. PAYLOAD, SIZE, ID and ADDR.
    4. PAYLOAD, SIZE, ID, ADDR and RS SIZE.
3. Testcase with ECC OFF and CRC ON.
Run each with 1k packets for first run. Make it 10k for regression later after verification.
tasks.creator: sumon

tasks.name: N3: AGC EXTRACTION SIM
tasks.description:
1. run rx\_fe +agc in schemaric (100uv , 200uv , 400uv , 800uv , 1.6mv ,3.2mv, 6.4mv, 12.8mv, 25.6mv, 51.2mv, 102.4mv)
2. run rx\_fe (schematic )+agc (extraction) in (100uv , 200uv , 400uv , 800uv , 1.6mv ,3.2mv, 6.4mv, 12.8mv, 25.6mv, 51.2mv, 102.4mv)

comments.author: Manash Dey

comments.text:
Delay reason:
The original due date was set for the completion of "N3: Top Level Initial Simulation." Subsequently, additional tests were incorporated to thoroughly evaluate new features with the "N3: Top Level Extensive Simulation with Regression Setup." @Shovan Maity 
Additional delays occurred due to the discovery of bugs and the need for repeated code changes to PS and FIFO.@Sayantan Dey 
The integration of assertions took longer than expected due to the absence of a clear solution to the problem. Timing checks were defined in terms of raw duration, but assertions could only check at the edges of the clock. This resulted in inaccurate time margins between signals across multiple clock domains.
Finally, due date of all tests with extensive regression runs was extended till 3rd Dec. @Pushpal Das @sumon
comments.author: Abhishek Sarkar

tasks.description:
Why: ps and phy rtl have been changed. Need to change the top-level netlist and validate few features .
Deliverable:

2. RTL Verification- Multiple Offset correction trigger without IC reset
3. RTL Verification- ps\_rssi\_cal\_dly\_count  has to be different from rssi\_en to peak reset low and peak reset low to rssi\_cal\_en

tasks.name: N3: [QS127M Bench] FIFO read write test - 100K with varying FIFO sizes (256, 512, 1024).
tasks.creator: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Delay reason : there was a couple of issues surfaced during the post silicon checkout. next steps to identify  on the path forward as a startegy took time and we have to update the scripts and redo the data collection and hence delayed.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text: Thanks @Anish Saha  for the note. As discussed in the teams chat, lets proceed with flushing the FIFO after the FIFO change and share the results here.
comments.author: Sarat Anumula

comments.text:
Task Update:  We have found some issues in the test; we are waiting for the stakeholders to get a final decision on the solution.
We had a discussion with Sayantan regarding the FIFO read/write failures, and we identified the root cause. The issue occurs because the FIFO size is being changed dynamically without flushing the FIFO beforehand. As a result, the FIFO pointers may end up pointing to undefined locations when the size changes.
To avoid this, we may need to flush the FIFO before starting the FIFO read/write rollover test.
There are two possible solutions:
1. Flush the FIFO before the start of each run — This would ensure the pointers are reset, although it may be somewhat excessive.
2. Change the FIFO size dynamically in increasing order (e.g., 256 → 512 → 1024) — In this case, the pointers are less likely to enter an invalid state since the FIFO size is expanding rather than shrinking. ( Note previously I was doing this in opposite order that's why we run into this problem first place.)
Note: In practical scenarios, the FIFO size would typically remain constant. Even if it does change, the FIFO would generally be flushed before modifying the size.
comments.author: Anish Saha

Hi @Sarat Anumula, this task couldn't be targeted today due to the TX carrier measurements task. As we defined the FFT captures and the drive strength sweep for the TX task only on Saturday evening, test cases have increased by more than 80x - across 7 different drive strengths, across 4 carriers across 3 Vbat voltages for both FFT and signal capture.

As the TX test data is important for the wearables and regulatory testing, I will come back to this Flash PLL ADC/FIFO read-write task once after the TX carrier task is complete

Hence, can you please change the due date to Thursday. Thank you.

This Task can be marked as completed.
Test Results: 

Here is a summary of the configurations tested. For all listed tests, the SPI Frequency was 9.6M, VDD_AON was 750 mV, and SYS_CLK was 5M, NO IDLE SLOT

Tested Configurations Summary
The tests utilized a combination of CRC, ECC, and DC Balance settings across various datarates and data sizes.
We are getting pmdr = 0 for all configs.

tasks.name: N3: [QS127M Bench] RO frequency calibration test.
tasks.description:
**Why:** Ixana plans to take QS127M into production. 

**Deliverable:** 
The following test is used to measure the digitized Ring oscillator frequency. The reference is **schedule clock (2.5MHz)**.
**Measured Parameters:** Status burst read 2 locations from 0x0462 for **ro\_cal\_done**, **cycle\_count\_ro\_cal\[14:8\], cycle\_count\_ro\_cal\[7:0\]**
**Expectation:** Calculated RO clk frequency should be **~32kHz**.

Detailed description of the test is documented here: See #16 See RO frequency calibration.
[https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-74597](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/docs/8cqezuv-60897/8cqezuv-74597)

**Risks (T,E):**

**Limitations (Assumptions (A), Constraints (C), Dependency (D))**
Assumption: FW is working as expected.

Results 

(T)iger is a risk that everybody understands
(E)lephant is a risk that I know but others don't think so, yet
tasks.creator: Sarat Anumula
